regulatory_references.name: NDS 2030
regulatory_references.relevance: National development framework — projects should align with NDS pillars

regulatory_references.name: QNV 2030
regulatory_references.relevance: Long-term national vision with four pillars: human, social, economic, environmental

regulatory_references.name: MOTC
regulatory_references.relevance: ICT and digital government regulations in Qatar

regulatory_references.name: QCB
regulatory_references.relevance: Financial sector regulations and fintech compliance

compliance_patterns.description: Government projects may require minimum Qatari national content
Qatar government typically requires bilingual deliverables
typical_requirements: All deliverables in Arabic and English

evaluator_insights.insight: Qatar public sector values international best practices adapted to local context
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals should reference international benchmarks with clear localization strategy
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include international case studies with explicit Qatar adaptation plan

evaluator_insights.insight: World Cup 2022 legacy projects influence procurement priorities
evaluator_insights.implication: Post-mega-event institutional capacity building is a common theme
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Reference institutional sustainability and legacy planning where relevant

evaluator_insights.insight: Technical excellence and innovation valued alongside compliance
evaluator_insights.implication: Methodology should demonstrate cutting-edge approaches, not just standard consulting
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include innovation elements: AI/analytics, digital tools, design thinking

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume Saudi regulations apply to Qatar — different regulatory framework
Do NOT reference Vision 2030 (Saudi) — Qatar uses QNV 2030 and NDS 2030
Do NOT assume Saudization — Qatar has Qatarization with different rules
Do NOT assume Arabic-only — Qatar often uses English as working language in government
